title: madhuca bourdillonii
intro: Madhuca bourdillonii, also known as Indian Butter Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to the Western Ghats of India. The medicinal properties of Madhuca bourdillonii include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Madhuca bourdillonii include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Madhuca bourdillonii is used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, and its bark and leaves are often used separately to prepare these remedies.
